pages.title: Overview
pages.summary: Track progress and workflow navigation for the supervisor.

pages.title: Raw Sources
pages.summary: Frozen untouched source packages and BRCA-window raw previews before any normalization.

pages.title: Normalization
pages.summary: Per-source BRCA normalization artifacts with explicit provenance and no live queries.

pages.title: Pre-GME Review
pages.summary: Arab-aware BRCA checkpoint from ClinVar + gnomAD + SHGP before GME is added.

pages.title: Final Registry
pages.summary: Arab-aware BRCA checkpoint after the supporting GME layer is added.

pages.title: Controlled Access
pages.summary: Official acquisition paths for restricted Arab datasets still outside the active workflow.

harmonization_steps.title: Step 1: Normalize ClinVar BRCA rows
harmonization_steps.simple: Extract the BRCA windows from the GRCh38 ClinVar VCF, prefix chromosomes with chr, then normalize alleles with bcftools.
harmonization_steps.technical: The normalized rows keep ALLELEID, CLNSIG, CLNREVSTAT, MC-derived effect labels, and raw-record lineage through the OLD_REC tag.

harmonization_steps.title: Step 2: Normalize gnomAD genomes BRCA rows
harmonization_steps.simple: Extract chr13 and chr17 BRCA windows from gnomAD genomes, then normalize biallelic alleles with bcftools.
harmonization_steps.technical: The artifact preserves AC, AN, AF, nhomalt, AFR, and MID metrics per normalized allele.

harmonization_steps.title: Step 3: Normalize gnomAD exomes BRCA rows
harmonization_steps.simple: Extract chr13 and chr17 BRCA windows from gnomAD exomes, then normalize biallelic alleles with bcftools.
harmonization_steps.technical: Exomes stay separate from genomes in the per-source artifact so later combined metrics remain auditable.

harmonization_steps.title: Step 4: Normalize SHGP BRCA rows
harmonization_steps.simple: Filter the Saudi frequency table to BRCA windows, convert rows to minimal VCF, then normalize with bcftools.
harmonization_steps.technical: Each row keeps its original table row locator and the derived SHGP AF still ties back to AC and AN.

harmonization_steps.title: Step 5: Build the Arab pre-GME checkpoint
harmonization_steps.simple: Join the normalized ClinVar, gnomAD, and SHGP artifacts on the canonical variant key.
harmonization_steps.technical: The required publication-facing header stays first and every combined gnomAD metric is derived from explicit per-cohort counts.

final_steps.title: Step 6: Normalize GME BRCA rows
final_steps.simple: Filter GME to BRCA windows, convert summary rows into minimal VCF, then normalize with bcftools.
final_steps.technical: Deletion rows that use '-' are anchored on the previous GRCh38 base so the VCF representation remains valid and explicit.

final_steps.title: Step 7: Build the final Arab checkpoint
final_steps.simple: Add the supporting GME layer on top of the Arab pre-GME checkpoint without changing the required header order.
final_steps.technical: Only the Arab-relevant GME fields are carried forward as extras, so context-only subgroup columns do not clutter the final registry.

pre_gme.title: supervisor_variant_registry_brca_arab_pre_gme_v2
pre_gme.scope_note: This checkpoint is the Arab-aware BRCA table before GME is added. It combines normalized ClinVar, gnomAD, and SHGP rows only.
accuracy_notes: The required publication-facing header remains the minimum schema and unsupported fields stay NULL.
Combined gnomAD fields are calculated from explicit genomes/exomes counts carried alongside the final row.
SHGP is now part of the pre-GME checkpoint because it is a primary Arab frequency source, not an afterthought.
scientific_notes: This table is built from frozen normalized Parquet artifacts only; there is no live BigQuery rebuild behind the UI.
Every row inherits the canonical variant key from the normalized source artifacts, and each source can still be reviewed independently on the Harmonization page.

columns.name: CHROM
columns.description: Canonical GRCh38 chromosome label with chr prefix.

columns.name: POS
columns.description: 1-based canonical start position.

columns.name: END
columns.description: Canonical end position after normalization.

columns.name: ID
columns.description: Stable pipeline identifier; here it is the canonical variant key.

columns.name: REF
columns.description: Normalized reference allele.

columns.name: ALT
columns.description: Normalized alternate allele.

columns.name: VARTYPE
columns.description: Variant class derived from the normalized REF/ALT pair.

columns.name: Repeat
columns.description: Reserved placeholder until a repeat-track source is added.

columns.name: Segdup
columns.description: Reserved placeholder until a segmental-duplication source is added.

columns.name: Blacklist
columns.description: Reserved placeholder until a blacklist-region source is added.

columns.name: GENE
columns.description: BRCA1 or BRCA2 assigned from the frozen Ensembl-backed windows.

columns.name: EFFECT

registry.title: supervisor_variant_registry_brca_arab_v2
registry.scope_note: This final checkpoint keeps the Arab-aware pre-GME table intact, then adds the supporting GME layer as explicit extras.
accuracy_notes: Context-only GME subgroup columns were intentionally left out of the final checkpoint to keep the Arab analysis focused.
The only added GME extras are the overall AF and the Arab-relevant subgroup frequencies (NWA, NEA, AP, SD).
scientific_notes: The final CSV download points to the frozen GCS export of this checkpoint, not a live query.
The same canonical variant key and per-source presence flags remain available for audit and downstream joins.
